channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access | APTA
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting challenges in workforce, access, and awareness.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres - Spinal Surgery News
item.description: The UK has launched a pioneering program to test the ARC-EX system, the first dedicated treatment for paralysis approved by UK regulators, which combines targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises to restore strength and movement in spinal cord injury patients.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions. The review included randomized clinical trials.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial demonstrated high agreement between physiotherapists, resident physicians, and senior physicians in diagnosing, treating, and discharging musculoskeletal conditions in an emergency department setting. This supports the implementation of Advanced Physiotherapy Practice roles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of 9 patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy was developed, encompassing functioning, psychosocial aspects, and satisfaction, suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study compared the accuracy of stride count estimation across multiple wearable sensor configurations (wrists, hips, ankles) and data processing algorithms in 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill across various walking speeds.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial provides an update to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, examining recent advances, current evidence, and persistent challenges in prevention, individualized management, surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying moderators associated with prevalence estimates by searching PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, and SportDiscus up to December 2024.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A thematic synthesis of 22 studies (n=645 informants) revealed profound psychological and social impacts of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, extending beyond physical impairment to affect emotional well-being and social participation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study (n=333) found that sarcopenia increased the risk of adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis (17 trials, n=1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.02, -0.42]; p < .01) in patients with knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis (n=NA) evaluated the impact of postpartum exercise on pelvic floor disorders and diastasis recti abdominis, providing evidence for its efficacy in managing these conditions.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients determined that admission Berg Balance Scale scores independently predict independent walking at discharge, and derived subtype-specific optimal cut-off values for ischemic and hemorrhagic stroke using multivariable logistic regression.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A cross-organizational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process generated recommendations for the tennis community on providing mental healthcare and promoting mental wellness among elite tennis athletes, including juniors, involving 83 participants from various governing bodies and the broader tennis ecosystem.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: This prospective observational study monitored 4450 athletes during the 14-day Paris 2024 Paralympic Games, reporting injury and illness epidemiology, including incidence (8.6 per 1000 athlete days), prevalence (10.9%), and sport-specific risks (e.g., Taekwondo 27.3).
item.author: BJSM

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement
item.description: This study aims to determine the effect of mandatory bundled payment for inpatient lower extremity joint replacement on the delivery of preoperative rehabilitation (prehabilitation).
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Improved Flexor Spasticity in Patients with Chronic Stroke Using a Dynamic Wrist-Hand Stretching Device: A Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A pilot RCT (n=30) found that a novel dynamic wrist-hand stretching device, Hand Stretcher®, significantly improved flexor spasticity and increased wrist range of motion in chronic stroke patients with mild-to-moderate spasticity over 8 weeks compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis evaluated the short-term and long-term therapeutic effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) on motor function in Parkinson's disease, identifying stimulation site, coil type, and stimulation frequency as key modulating factors.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review established weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury by analyzing studies from PubMed, Sportdiscuss, WOS, and Scielo databases, aiming to address the high recurrence rate due to lack of consensus.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Preoperative physiotherapy and one-year patient-reported outcomes after primary total knee arthroplasty: a registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients.
item.description: A registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients found no association between preoperative physiotherapy and improved one-year patient-reported outcomes or pain after primary total knee arthroplasty.
item.author: PubMed
